prompt:
Firmware portability ticket. Our telemetry frames match the deployed byte layout, but their CRC changes when the same source is built with a different structure-packing mode. The semantic sample object is a host-side type and its padding is not part of the protocol. Repair frame encoding so the CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE is computed over exactly the serialized wire bytes defined by the v1 frame: sync through status, excluding the checksum field itself. Do not checksum host object storage.

The v1 wire ABI is frozen. Preserve its packed size and offsets, the mixed byte order already used by the encoder (sequence big-endian, millivolts and CRC little-endian), and the byte-for-byte golden frames. Semantically identical samples must encode identically regardless of host padding contents or whether the compiler globally packs ordinary structs. Keep the checksum validator and public API behavior intact.

Run `make test`. Makefile, include/telemetry.h, and test_checksum_layout.c are protected; do not modify them. The build is local, has no dependencies, and deliberately runs the suite in default and globally packed host-structure modes.

full_distill.primary_skill: separate host object representation from the packed wire representation when computing a protocol checksum while retaining the frozen ABI and explicit byte order
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits nonzero because telemetry_encode feeds the padded, native-endian telemetry_sample object to CRC-16 instead of the nine serialized v1 bytes, so the golden frame and checksum validation fail in both compiler modes
full_distill.success_contract: default-layout and globally packed-host builds produce the same valid eleven-byte golden frames, changes confined to host padding cannot affect them, sequence/millivolts/CRC byte order remains pinned, and all protected sizeof/offsetof assertions continue to compile
adversarial_factors: the protected suite poisons padding in two semantically equal host samples, rejecting fixes that clear one object or rely on incidental zero-filled stack storage
the suite is compiled both normally and with -fpack-struct=1, so hard-coded native struct sizes and padding-dependent checksum spans cannot satisfy both modes

multiple golden frames pin a big-endian sequence beside little-endian millivolts and CRC, rejecting native scalar storage, sample-specific constants, and accidental success when the host type has no padding
protected static assertions pin every deployed frame offset and its total size, while corruption checks exercise the existing validator, rejecting wire-layout edits or validation bypasses
